$comment: Neutral shipped <stdint.h> (C99 7.18) — exact-width, minimum-width, fastest-minimum-width, pointer-width, and greatest-width integer typedefs expressed as language-NEUTRAL hir-text aliases, injected by the semantic phase (a C `.h` would be C-syntax TEXT and couple the shipped config to C; see README + limits.json). FIRST real consumer of the FC14 typedef-injection path. The pointer-width forms (intptr_t/uintptr_t) and the greatest-width forms (intmax_t/uintmax_t) are 8 bytes on EVERY current DSS target — all are 64-bit, and LP64 + LLP64 agree on pointer width (only `long` differs, which stdint does not expose) — so they are u64/i64. An ILP32 target would make them 4-byte: deferred D-FFI-STDINT-PTR-WIDTH-ILP32. The fastest-minimum-width forms are implementation-defined; mapping them to their exact-width minimum (int_fast16_t -> i16) is conforming (>= N bits) and keeps SQLite's self-contained amalgamation internally consistent. No `symbols` (no link surface); the *_MAX/*_MIN limit macros are a future `constants` pass (D-FFI-STDINT-LIMIT-MACROS).
